_doc: Cross-cutting taxonomy: concepts that appear in all three camps but carry different interpretations. These nodes are referenced by all three POV taxonomy files.

nodes.label: AGI Timelines
nodes.description: When will Artificial General Intelligence be achieved? All three camps reference this; interpretations diverge radically on both the timeline and its implications.

interpretations.accelerationist: AGI within 5-10 years; an urgent reason to invest now and shape its development.
interpretations.safetyist: AGI within 5-10 years; an urgent reason for alignment research before capability overhang.
interpretations.skeptic: AGI timelines are speculative hype that distracts from present, measurable harms.

nodes.label: The Alignment Problem
nodes.description: The challenge of ensuring AI systems reliably pursue intended goals. Central to all camps; urgency and solvability are contested.

interpretations.accelerationist: A real but tractable engineering problem; solving it is one more reason to move fast with capable labs leading.
interpretations.safetyist: An existential-level unsolved problem that must be resolved before further capability deployment.
interpretations.skeptic: A useful framing for future risk that is used to avoid accountability for alignment failures happening today (bias, misuse).

nodes.label: AI Governance and Regulation
nodes.description: All camps want 'good governance'; their definitions of what that means are largely incompatible.

interpretations.accelerationist: Light-touch regulation that does not stifle innovation; industry self-governance preferred.
interpretations.safetyist: Strong international coordination to pause or constrain frontier model development.
interpretations.skeptic: Immediate domestic regulation targeting deployed systems and their discriminatory effects.

nodes.label: Harm
nodes.description: Core vocabulary used by all three camps. The word 'harm' means entirely different things across POVs — different time horizons, different subjects, different severity thresholds.

interpretations.accelerationist: Harm = failing to deploy AI, thereby allowing preventable disease/poverty to continue.
interpretations.safetyist: Harm = catastrophic or irreversible outcomes from misaligned advanced AI.
interpretations.skeptic: Harm = documented, present-day discriminatory outcomes and labor displacement affecting real people now.

nodes.label: Risk
nodes.description: Same word; radically different probability estimates, time horizons, and moral weight across camps.

interpretations.accelerationist: Risk = low-probability tail events; present opportunity cost of not deploying AI is the dominant risk.
interpretations.safetyist: Risk = high-probability catastrophic outcome if alignment is unsolved before AGI; expected value dominates.
interpretations.skeptic: Risk = near-certain ongoing harm to marginalized groups from currently deployed systems.
